hm_viggo.surface_form: What is that you find fun about single-player games?
Nightshade is a 2017 single-player text adventure game developed by Red Entertainment Corporation.
So have you ever played a game that has a side view perspective, like for example Tetris?
Rollcage is an action-racing game for the PlayStation and PC. It's not available on Steam, Mac, or Linux.
I remember you said you disliked Assassin's Creed Chronicles: India. Are you not fond of action-adventure games featuring platforming from 2016 as a whole?
Little Nightmares is a good adventure puzzle platformer for PlayStation, Xbox, and PC. You can get the game on Steam as well.
Rise of the Tomb Raider is a 2015 action-adventure first person and third person shooter. It was developed by Crystal Dynamics for PlayStation, Xbox, PC, and Steam.
Do you mean the Little Big Adventure that Adeline Software International developed and released in 1994?
God of War was released in 2018 for PlayStation. It's an excellent action-adventure, platformer, role-playing game that is played in third person. There is no multiplayer mode.

Battlefield V is an action shooter game developed by EA Digital Illusions CE that's being released on October 16, 2018. You play this from a first person perspective, and there is multiplayer involved as well.
Tetris is a strategic puzzler arcade game with a side view released in 1986.
Do you mean Ori and the Blind Forest on the Xbox?
RollerCoaster Tycoon is a simulation strategy game with a bird view. It doesn't have multiplayer.

slots.name: Age of Empires II: The Age of Kings

hm_viggo.surface_form: Age of Empires II: The Age of Kings is a good real-time strategy game developed by Ensemble Studios in 1999. You play this from a bird view perspective.

slots.name: The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time

hm_viggo.surface_form: Among E rated games released back in 1998, one of my favorites is The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time. Are you familiar with it?
Sid Meier's Civilization V is a simulation turn-based strategy game with a bird view that was released in 2010.
You said that Assassin's Creed II was pretty fun. In general, do you enjoy playing those action-adventure platformers that are available for PlayStation?
StarCraft is a good real-time strategy game for PC. It is available for Mac but not for Linux. It is unavailable on Steam.
In 2018 a multiplayer action game filled with adventure released called A Way Out. It has gotten good ratings and is played as a third person.
Another excellent game is Portal 2 from 2011. It's a multiplayer puzzle-platformer FPS.
Tomb Raider: The Last Revelation is a solid action-adventure game released in 1999. The single-player experience combines third person shooter mechanics with puzzle solving.
BioShock is a good role-playing action-adventure first person shooter game that is rated M.
I was wondering, are you fond of the games developed by EA Digital Illusions CE that have multiplayer, such as Mirror's Edge Catalyst?
What other E rated game can you recommend?
Call of Duty: Advanced Warfare is an action shooter from 2014 for the PlayStation, Xbox, and PC through Steam.

slots.name: The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time

hm_viggo.surface_form: In 1998 Nintendo EAD released The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time on their Nintendo platform. It redefined the third person bird's eye action-adventure puzzle RPG genre.
Nightshade is an example of a bad text adventure that was released in 2017. It's only available on PC, with no Mac or Linux support.
Payday 2 from 2013 is a role-playing shooter with multiplayer.

slots.name: Age of Empires II: The Age of Kings

hm_viggo.surface_form: Age of Empires II: The Age of Kings, is a 1999 adventure game with real-time strategy. The developer Ensemble Studios ensures that the player perspective displays in bird view. Therefore, the user experience is very good.
In 2004, was there any game released that you felt was fascinating?
Little Nightmares is an adventure puzzle platformer that is out on PlayStation, Xbox, PC, and Steam.
Is it the PC game, The Elder Scrolls Online, which was developed by ZeniMax Online Studios?
StarCraft is a real-time strategy developed by Blizzard Entertainment. It has a T (for Teen) rating.
Can you think of any lengthy single-player games without a multiplayer component?
Why do you feel that first person role-playing shooters are typically neither bad nor great?
I rather enjoyed BioShock. Developer 2K Boston makes solid quality games, and the M rating gave it some nice grittyness.
The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt is an excellent game that was released in 2015. It is an M-rated third person action-adventure role-playing game.
Madden NFL 15 is a good game for a bird view perspective game, but not great.
Portal 2 is a 2011 multiplayer puzzle-shooter platformer. It is available on Steam, but was originally released for PlayStation and PC.
So what is it about action-adventure platformers that makes you enjoy them?
And for yet another game that came out in 2014 that you might be interested in, have you ever heard of Madden NFL 15?
2015's indie adventure RPG, Undertale, is that correct?
Multiplayer games for the PC are always at least a little fun, so I've been enjoying playing Diablo II lately.
MotorStorm: Apocalypse is a vehicular combat rated T (for Teen) that was released for PlayStation in 2011. It got a poor reception.

The excellent indie adventure game Hellblade: Senua's Sacrifice features hack-and-slash action. This PlayStation, Xbox and PC game that was released in 2017 has no Linux or Mac compatibility but is available on Steam.
Are you into third person PC games like Little Big Adventure?
Why do you say that Evolution Studios' vehicular combat games are so bad?
BioShock was good. It was released in 2007 on PlayStation, Xbox,PC, and Mac, but no Linux. It's an action-adventure, role-playing and shooter game, and you can find it on Steam.

Mirror's Edge Catalyst is a first person action-adventure fighting game with a multiplayer mode. It's playable on PlayStation, Xbox, and PC. It's not available on Steam or playable on Mac, but it is playable on Linux.
Can you recommend any smart third person games?
Metro 2033 is a good action, role-playing, shooter game. There is no multiplayer mode. The game is available for Xbox and PC, as well as Steam, but there was no release for Linux or Mac.
You told me you didn't really like Need for Speed: Payback. But would your opinion change if it didn't have that third person perspective and a teen rating?
Metroid Prime Pinball is an odd little game to boot up on your Nintendo, but the multiplayer game does give an average experience all in all.
So Mass Effect 2 is a game you find really excellent, I remember you mentioning. Would you say that, in general, you love the M rated games that are there on PlayStation?
